title: No network access with CNI 0.8
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

No network access in container


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo podman run -it alpine wget http://192.30.253.112/containers/libpod`

(ip address of github, since using the domain immediately fails with `wget: bad address 'github.com'`, so DNS not so much here).

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Connecting to 192.30.253.112 (192.30.253.112:80)
wget: can't connect to remote host (192.30.253.112): Operation timed out
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
wget http://192.30.253.112/containers/libpod
--2019-05-31 21:53:28--  http://192.30.253.112/containers/libpod
Connecting to 192.30.253.112:80... connected.
HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 301 Moved Permanently
Location: https://192.30.253.112/containers/libpod [following]
--2019-05-31 21:53:28--  https://192.30.253.112/containers/libpod
Loaded CA certificate '/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt'
Connecting to 192.30.253.112:443... connected.
The certificate's owner does not match hostname ‘192.30.253.112’
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I've got nothing special configured or settings changed here. This is pretty much fresh off podman installation and just trying to do the basics. No firewalld. Not trying rootless here. Using `--net=host` does enable access. But somewhat defeats the purpose of contained networking. I just can't figure out what's going on here


```
$ podman version
Version:            1.3.2
$ buildah version
Version:         1.8.2
$ sudo pacman -Qi cni-plugins 
Name            : cni-plugins
Version         : 0.8.0-1
$ uname -rm
5.1.5-arch1-2-ARCH x86_64
```

Here is the debug log of running the container:

```

physical machine

comments.body:
Hm. It looks like CNI is coming up properly... IPTables rules definitely being added. Nothing seems obviously wrong...

Could be a missing sysctl. `net.ipv4.ip_forward` not being set maybe?

looks to be set:

```
$ sudo sysctl net.ipv4.ip_forward
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1
```

thanks for taking a look

There are a few more we need - I'll try and find the exact list on Monday.

Also, let me tag in a CNI expert - @mccv1r0 - mind taking a look here?
also havent we had issues with cni 0.8.x @mheon

Oooh, thanks for the hint. Downgraded to cni to 0.7.5, and that did fix things:

```
$ sudo podman run -it alpine ping 8.8.8.8
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: seq=0 ttl=55 time=6.581 ms
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: seq=1 ttl=55 time=6.199 ms
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: seq=2 ttl=55 time=9.876 ms
```

and no problem with dns:

```
$ sudo podman run -it alpine wget https://github.com/containers/libpod
Connecting to github.com (192.30.253.112:443)
libpod               100% |*********************************************************************|  150k  0:00:00 ETA
```

So there does seem to be a problem with cni 0.8. Happy to help find any other logs or track down what's going on there. 

Thank you for that tip off though.
Can your CNI conflist be provided.  I can play wtih exactly what you are using while I setup for ```podman 1.3.2```

Maybe something to narrow search:
I am also on Arch, with only difference being that I use vfs driver instead of overlay and I run my container rootless and I use nftables instead of iptables. No problems with network here:
```
$ podman run --rm -it alpine wget http://github.com/containers/libpod
Connecting to github.com (140.82.118.4:80)
Connecting to github.com (140.82.118.4:443)
libpod               100% |************************************************|  149k  0:00:00 ETA
```
packages for confirmation:
```
local/podman 1.3.2-1
local/cni-plugins 0.8.0-1
```
info:
```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.5
  podman version: 1.3.2

and `/etc/cni/*`is the same as in previous post.
Ah, you're not using CNI if you're running rootless - we use `slirp4netns` to manage rootless networking.
Closing, as fixed upstream by CNI.
